semantics.purpose: Preregistered synthetic evidence for report-only Java test-integrity detector research.

signals.name: empty-or-assertionless-junit-test
signals.definition: A JUnit-shaped test method without assertion or interaction verification.

signals.name: disabled-junit-test-without-reason
signals.definition: A JUnit-shaped disabled test without a usable reason.

records.title: Empty JUnit-shaped test
records.auditEvidence: synthetic source-only positive for an assertionless test method
records.threatCapability: A local detector consumer can overstate a structural finding as product evidence.
attackPreconditions: The detector input is a synthetic local fixture.
No PH command or external issuer is involved.
records.desiredInvariant: A report-only finding remains diagnostic and cannot authorize workflow finish.
records.futureAcceptanceBoundary: Candidate output must remain untrusted until an independently authorized path exists.

records.title: JUnit-shaped test with assertion
records.auditEvidence: synthetic source-only negative with explicit assertion call
records.threatCapability: A lexical detector can misclassify assertion-bearing tests.
attackPreconditions: The assertion is present in the synthetic fixture.
No runtime test execution is performed.
records.desiredInvariant: The evaluator preserves the preregistered negative label.
records.futureAcceptanceBoundary: Negative labels cannot be changed after evaluation.

records.title: Disabled JUnit-shaped test without reason
records.auditEvidence: synthetic source-only positive for a disabled test without reason
records.threatCapability: A report-only finding can be mistaken for an enforcement decision.
attackPreconditions: The disabled annotation has no reason in the synthetic fixture.
No product workflow state is read.
records.desiredInvariant: The result is diagnostic-only and cannot block, force, or promote a workflow.
records.futureAcceptanceBoundary: A future policy must separately authorize any enforcement behavior.

records.title: Disabled JUnit-shaped test with reason
records.auditEvidence: synthetic source-only negative with an intentional disabled reason
records.threatCapability: A detector can mistake any disabled annotation for an integrity warning.
attackPreconditions: The annotation includes a non-empty reason.
No runtime test execution is performed.
records.desiredInvariant: The preregistered negative remains immutable and diagnostic.
records.futureAcceptanceBoundary: Any later change requires a new corpus version and result.
